claim: Software engineers must shift from writing code to building systems that write code; this is a natural progression like OOP and microservices abstractions
actionable: Reframe your engineering role as system design around AI agents; focus on prompt structure, workflow design, and harness quality rather than line-by-line code
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Andrew Filev: Multi Model Pipelines

claim: Using Opus for all implementation costs ~$2000/engineer/month; cheaper models for implementation with Opus planning achieves 60-80% cost reduction with same quality
actionable: In plan-implement workflows: use Opus (or best available) for planning, use cheaper models (Gemini Flash, Codex 5.3, open-source) for implementation to reduce token spend
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Andrew Filev: Multi Model Pipelines

claim: Model diversity in multi-model pipelines (e.g., planning with Opus, implementing with Gemini) produces better results than single-model pipelines despite lower cost
actionable: Mix different models in your pipeline: different models have different perspectives which can catch bugs and improve solution quality unexpectedly
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Andrew Filev: Multi Model Pipelines

claim: AI review using multiple different models (e.g., Cortex + Gemini + single Opus pass) costs $0.25 vs Claude's review bot at $12-20 per PR with better precision/recall
actionable: Build custom multi-model code review pipelines instead of using single-model PR review bots; use different models for different review checks
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Andrew Filev: Multi Model Pipelines

claim: Coding is essentially solved by LLMs if you give them a good plan; the leverage is in planning and specification, not implementation iteration
actionable: Spend most of your effort on writing detailed implementation plans; most models will execute them correctly regardless of model tier
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Andrew Filev: Multi Model Pipelines
